pattern: A grid that only allows right and down moves collapses to a simple dynamic program where each cell's best cost combines its entrance fee with the cheaper of the two incoming neighbours plus that neighbour's leaving wait.
hints: Because you may only ever move right or down, the cost to first stand on a cell depends solely on the two cells above and to its left.
Entering a cell costs (i+1)*(j+1); leaving any cell other than the origin first costs that cell's wait. Fold the leaving wait into the transition.
Let f[i][j] be the least cost to enter cell (i,j). Then f[i][j] = entry(i,j) + min(f[i-1][j] + wait[i-1][j], f[i][j-1] + wait[i][j-1]), with f[0][0] = 1 and no wait charged when leaving the origin.

edge_inputs.description: two by two grid with a wait
zero waits everywhere isolates entry costs
minimum size grid of two cells

stress.description: moderate rectangular grid forcing the full DP
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size // 4) + 1)
    n = max(2, (max(2, size) + m - 1) // m)
    wait = [[rng.randrange(0, 100001) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [m, n, wait]
